title: CLAUDE.md — working guide for intraclass
text: Oracle-first (#1): correctness established numerical agreement ≥2 independent oracles, never formula “looking right”. Name estimand coding (#2, #14): plan code; respect milestone gates; thin vertical slices. Intervals Monte-Carlo boundary-aware (#3): never point estimate without interval; report method. fabricated reference values (#4): cited source committed seeded script. Fail loudly ill-posed designs (#5) via classed abort_*() layer. user messaging via cli; errors via rlang::abort() classed (#8). bare stop()/warning()/cat()/print(). Tracking files update commit work (#16). Format air committing (air format .); CI enforces air format --check. lintr owns semantic linters . See ADR-004.

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R
text: Build R package operationalizes generalizability-theory (GT) framework intraclass correlation coefficients (ICCs) developed ten Hove, Jorgensen & van der Ark, using modern variance-component estimation rather classical ANOVA / McGraw–Wong mean-squares approach. package exist (differentiators): - Existing tools split two camps. classical ones (psych, irr, irrNA, irrICC, ICCDesign) ANOVA / mean-squares based. assume balanced data, though tolerate missing cells — irrICC (Gwet) irrNA compute ICCs wide rating matrix NAs, closest prior art incomplete designs, still within classical model rather mixed-model / MC-CI approach. model-based ones (performance::icc, gtheory, misty) extract variance-partition coefficient fitted model implement full interrater-reliability ICC family (absolute agreement vs. consistency × single vs. average × fixed vs. random raters), error-variance framing, selection framework. - GT framework’s reference code loose supplementary scripts, engineered package. - gap fill: coherent, well-tested package (1) fits variance components modern engines, (2) computes correct ICC stated design proper interval estimation, (3) handles imbalanced / incomplete / multilevel designs, (4) teaches user ICC choose . Audience: applied researchers psychology, education, medicine, behavioral science need defensible IRR estimates guidance. first-class goal, afterthought: package pkgdown site place learn ICC best practices. Every estimator’s docs every vignette must explain estimand, assumptions, tradeoffs behind “knob,” guide reader sensible choice. Estimation engines (dependency discipline): - Default engine: lme4 /glmmTMB (MLE random-effects models). framework’s 2025 simulation work favors MLE-RE Monte-Carlo CIs feasibility accuracy, spine. - Optional engines (Suggests, never Imports): brms / rstanarm (Bayesian) lavaan / blavaan (SEM / common-factor). Keep base install light fast; gate optional-engine code behind rlang::check_installed().

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R
text: load-bearing. Encode verbatim PRINCIPLES.md. Changing principle requires explicit approval dated entry DECISIONS.md.

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R
text: Oracle-first verification. estimator considered correct formula “looks right” model reasoned . Correctness established numerical agreement independent oracles: () closed-form / textbook values (e.g., Brennan 2001, Shrout & Fleiss 1979 worked examples), (b) least one established package balanced case supports (psych::ICC, gtheory), (c) simulation known population variance components. Every exported estimator must pass ≥2 independent oracle types. Name estimand. Every ICC function documents precisely population quantity estimates (variance components numerator/denominator, single vs. average, agreement vs. consistency, fixed vs. random raters) code written. Interval estimation engine-agnostic boundary-aware. Default Monte-Carlo CIs simulated parameter covariance matrix (ICC non-normal ratio; delta method unreliable near zero-rater-variance boundary, common case). Report method used. Never present point estimate without interval unless explicitly asked. fabricated reference values, ever. Oracle values tests must come cited source reproducible script committed repo (seed). value sourced reproduced, test written estimator shipped. Fail loudly ill-posed designs. requested ICC identified supplied design (e.g., variance component separated), error classed, explanatory condition — silently return plausible-looking number.

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R

Progress, warnings, informative notes use cli. errors use rlang::abort() classed condition actionable message. bare stop()/warning()/cat()/print() user communication. Pure functions, explicit state. reliance global options correctness; writing user’s filesystem .GlobalEnv side effect.

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R
text: Scaffold usethis; prefer generating config via usethis/use_* helpers versions stay current. Package skeleton: usethis::create_package(), MIT license (confirm ), use_readme_rmd(), use_news_md(), use_lifecycle() badges. Testing: testthat 3rd edition (use_testthat(3)), snapshot tests, use_coverage() (covr → Codecov). Messaging & conditions: cli messaging; rlang classed conditions errors/warnings. Add small internal abort_*() helper layer. Generics: generics (tidy/glance/augment); ggplot2::autoplot methods plot aids interpretation. Docs: roxygen2 markdown enabled; use_package_doc(); spell check (use_spell_check(), maintain inst/WORDLIST). Getting started — fit → ICC → interpret, clean balanced example. Choosing ICC — decision framework: agreement vs. consistency, single vs. average, fixed vs. random raters, complete vs. incomplete, subject- vs. cluster-level. flagship teaching article. Advanced / imbalanced & multilevel — incomplete designs, multilevel ICCs, engine choice (LMM vs. SEM vs. Bayesian) matters, CI methods. CI (GitHub Actions via usethis::use_github_action()): R-CMD-check (matrix: release + devel + oldrel, least Linux + macOS + Windows), test-coverage, lint, pkgdown deploy release/main. Add scheduled reference-values job reruns oracle/simulation scripts diffs committed expectations. Style & hygiene: lintr + air (styler) formatting; use_pre_commit() use pre-commit; use_tidy_description(). Dependency discipline: minimal Imports; optional engines heavy deps Suggests rlang::check_installed() guards; document light-install path.

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R
text: Create project/ directory (committed) single source truth. Keep CLAUDE.md lean — mostly point files skills §5, CLAUDE.md paid every turn load demand. Conventions agents must follow: - STATUS.md short structured (current milestone, active task, last green-CI commit, blockers). updated every task transition. - Every non-trivial statistical API decision gets DECISIONS.md entry (ADR format: context → decision → consequences → references). - REFERENCES.md links oracle value test suite source (citation committed script + seed). - Tracking updates ship commit work describe. Seed templates (create bootstrap): STATUS.md DECISIONS.md entry template MILESTONES.md entry template
code: project/ ├── PRINCIPLES.md     # §2 verbatim. Change-controlled. ├── ROADMAP.md        # long-range vision + proposed (not-yet-scheduled) work ├── MILESTONES.md     # ordered milestones, each with Definition of Done + status ├── TASKS.md          # current-milestone task board (checkbox list, one owner-agent each) ├── STATUS.md         # compact current-state snapshot the skills read/update ├── DECISIONS.md      # ADR log: dated statistical & architectural decisions + rationale + refs └── REFERENCES.md     # bibliography + registry of oracle values used in tests, with provenance # Project status - Milestone: M0 — scaffolding (not started) - Active task: — - Last green CI: — - Blockers: — - Updated: <date> by <agent> ## ADR-000: <title> - Date: <yyyy-mm-dd> - Status: proposed | accepted | superseded - Context: <why this came up> - Decision: <what we chose> - Consequences: <tradeoffs, what it rules out> - References: <paper + equation / issue / package> ## M<n>: <name> - Goal: <one sentence> - Definition of Done: <checklist, see §8> - Status: not started | in progress | done (commit <sha>)

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R
text: Create .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (folder per skill, YAML frontmatter + markdown body). auto-loadable invocable /name. Descriptions trigger — write concretely. Flesh bodies bootstrap following specs. status — “Report current project state next.” Reads STATUS.md + MILESTONES.md + TASKS.md; prints current milestone, active task, blockers, next action. Read-. start-task — “Begin next task board.” Selects next unblocked task, restates acceptance criteria specific PRINCIPLES must honor, sets STATUS.md -progress, outlines plan editing code. finish-task — “Close current task.” Runs devtools::check(), tests, lint, coverage; green, updates TASKS.md/STATUS.md, adds DECISIONS.md entry decision made, proposes conventional commit message. Never marks done red. verify-estimator — “Verify statistical estimator oracles.” Implements §2 principle 1: assembles oracle set (textbook/analytic, established package, simulation), runs comparison Opus, reports agreement tolerance. Writes results REFERENCES.md. result pinned oracle, escalate — surfaces gap recommends approve Fable review (§6, principle 19), stops. new-estimator — “Scaffold new ICC estimator.” Generates function stub, documented estimand block, test skeleton required oracle checklist, DECISIONS.md stub. Refuses leave estimator without oracle tests. add-decision — “Record architectural/statistical decision.” Appends filled ADR DECISIONS.md. Example SKILL.md skeleton (.claude/skills/status/SKILL.md):
code: --- name: status description: Report current project state and the next action. Use when I ask "where are we", "what's next", or "status". allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob --- ## Current state !`cat project/STATUS.md`  ## Instructions Summarize the active milestone and task, list blockers, and state the single next action. Do not modify files. If STATUS.md is stale relative to git history, say so.

title: Kickoff Brief — intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients in R
text: Baseline: Opus. Run main session Opus default subagents Opus. Encode policy CLAUDE.md per-subagent model: frontmatter. Subagents (auto-delegable) Opus Sonnet . Encode .claude/agents/. Example: Fable manual, gated escalation — agent. create Fable subagent (auto-delegation driven description field, Fable subagent fire burn tokens; established subagent can even run session’s model tier). Instead, verify-estimator reports result pinned oracle, presents gap stops. approve Fable review, run deliberately — switching session model /model opening separate Fable session — using checklist reviewer prompt: Statistical review (Fable). Correctness established numerical oracles, assertion. (1) Check derivation assumptions cited source equation. (2) Identify boundary/degenerate cases current oracles miss (e.g., zero rater variance, single rater, fully incomplete rows). (3) Flag anything unproven name specific oracle settle . approve result lacks oracle. Record outcome (fact Fable used) DECISIONS.md. Two honesty caveats — skip: - Review ≠ smarter model. capable model can reason correctness establish . Oracle tests (§2.1) remain source truth; Fable review stress-tests finds gaps, certify. - Fable availability unconfirmed , Mythos-tier models safeguards mechanism can route queries Opus 4.8. Confirm resolves via /model relying ; doesn’t, review Opus note DECISIONS.md. Note: built-Explore agent inherits main model, capped Opus, Opus main session exploration falls Haiku — consistent policy.
code: --- name: doc-polisher description: Tidies roxygen, NEWS, and prose. Use for low-risk documentation edits only. tools: Read, Edit, Grep, Glob model: sonnet --- You tidy documentation without changing behavior or public API. Never edit R logic or tests.

title: Advanced: imbalanced & multilevel designs
text: article cover mixed-model approach earns keep: Incomplete imbalanced designs — missing rater × subject cells, classical ANOVA identities fail mixed-model path valid. Multilevel ICCs — subject-level vs. cluster-level coefficients. Engine choice — linear mixed models vs. SEM vs. Bayesian, matters. Confidence-interval methods — Monte-Carlo (default) alternatives, especially near zero-rater-variance boundary. placeholder, filled across Milestones 3–5 (project/MILESTONES.md).

title: Choosing an ICC
text: flagship teaching article. guide choices define ICC need: Absolute agreement vs. consistency — actual value matter, rank order? Single vs. average — reporting one rater, mean k? Fixed vs. random raters — raters whole population interest, sample generalize ? Complete vs. incomplete — every subject rated every rater? Subject- vs. cluster-level — object measurement sit? placeholder. full decision framework written Milestone 3 (project/MILESTONES.md), milestone incomplete designs make choice consequential.

title: Getting started
text: use classic Shrout & Fleiss (1979) example: 6 subjects rated 4 raters, one rating per cell. intraclass wants long format — one rating per row.
code: ratings <- data.frame(   subject = factor(rep(1:6, times = 4)),   rater = factor(rep(1:4, each = 6)),   score = c(     9, 6, 8, 7, 10, 6, # rater 1     2, 1, 4, 1, 5, 2, # rater 2     5, 3, 6, 2, 6, 4, # rater 3     8, 2, 8, 6, 9, 7 # rater 4   ) ) head(ratings) #>   subject rater score #> 1       1     1     9 #> 2       2     1     6 #> 3       3     1     8 #> 4       4     1     7 #> 5       5     1    10 #> 6       6     1     6

title: Getting started
text: Call icc() data three columns (unquoted). defaults give two-way random, absolute-agreement coefficients ICC(,1) ICC(,k). set seed Monte-Carlo confidence interval reproducible.
code: fit <- icc(ratings, score, subject, rater, seed = 2024) fit #> # Intraclass correlation: two-way random, absolute agreement #> Subjects: 6 | Raters: 4 (random) | Observations: 24 #> Engine: glmmTMB (REML) | CI: 95% montecarlo (10000 draws) #>   index     estimate   95% CI #>   ICC(A,1)    0.290   [0.050, 0.711] #>   ICC(A,k)    0.620   [0.173, 0.908] #> Variance components: subject 2.556, rater 5.244, residual 1.019

what: Read the result with the tidy verbs
title: Getting started
text: tidy() returns one row per coefficient, estimate confidence interval; glance() returns one-row model summary including variance components.
code: tidy(fit) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   index    estimate std.error conf.low conf.high conf.level method     #>   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> <chr>      #> 1 ICC(A,1)    0.290     0.180   0.0498     0.711       0.95 montecarlo #> 2 ICC(A,k)    0.620     0.201   0.173      0.908       0.95 montecarlo  glance(fit) #> # A tibble: 1 × 9 #>   n_subjects n_raters n_obs var_subject var_rater var_residual engine  ci_method #>        <int>    <int> <int>       <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl> <chr>   <chr>     #> 1          6        4    24        2.56      5.24         1.02 glmmTMB montecar… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: conf.level <dbl>

title: Getting started
text: ICC(,1) ≈ 0.29 reliability single rater; ICC(,k) ≈ 0.62 reliability mean 4 raters. Averaging raises reliability (Spearman–Brown), report ICC(,k) averaged score actually use. absolute-agreement coefficients: systematic differences rater level (raters scoring higher others) count error. raters differ sharply average level, agreement ICC much lower consistency ICC — signal rating procedure level problem worth fixing. confidence interval Monte-Carlo boundary-aware: simulated fitted parameter covariance model’s internal scale, behaves near common zero-rater-variance boundary delta method fails.

title: Getting started
text: Absolute agreement vs. consistency, single vs. average, fixed vs. random raters, complete vs. incomplete designs — choices define ICC correct study. dedicated “Choosing ICC” article covers decision depth.

title: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients
text: intraclass computes interrater-reliability intraclass correlation coefficients (ICCs) within generalizability-theory framework, using modern variance-component estimation (linear mixed models) rather classical ANOVA / mean-squares approach. aims (1) fit variance components modern engines, (2) compute correct ICC stated design proper (boundary-aware Monte-Carlo) interval estimation, (3) handle imbalanced, incomplete, multilevel designs, (4) help decide ICC choose, — docs website place learn ICC best practice, just call functions. [!NOTE] package early development. first estimator — two-way random, absolute-agreement ICC(,1) / ICC(,k) — current milestone (M1). See project/MILESTONES.md roadmap.

title: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients
text: can install development version GitHub : base install light — glmmTMB, cli, rlang, generics. Optional Bayesian SEM engines live Suggests.

title: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients
code: library(intraclass) ## The icc() interface lands in Milestone 1: ## icc(data, score, subject, rater, type = "agreement", unit = c("single", "average"))

title: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients
text: Classical tools (psych, irr, irrNA, irrICC, ICCDesign) ANOVA / mean-squares based mostly assume balanced data; model-based tools (performance::icc, gtheory, misty) extract variance-partition coefficient full interrater-reliability ICC family, error-variance framing, selection framework. intraclass fills gap mixed-model estimation, Monte-Carlo confidence intervals, decision guidance (following ten Hove, Jorgensen & van der Ark).

what: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
text: Estimates interrater-reliability intraclass correlation coefficients (ICCs) fitted linear mixed model, rather classical ANOVA mean squares. release icc() computes two-way random, absolute-agreement coefficients ICC(,1) ICC(,k) (McGraw & Wong 1996), equivalent Shrout & Fleiss (1979) ICC(2,1) ICC(2,k).

title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
code: # S3 method for class 'icc' format(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'icc' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'icc' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'icc' tidy(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'icc' glance(x, ...)  icc(   data,   score,   subject,   rater,   model = "twoway",   type = "agreement",   unit = c("single", "average"),   engine = "glmmTMB",   conf_level = 0.95,   ci_method = "montecarlo",   mc_samples = 10000L,   seed = NULL )

title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
text: x, object icc object. ... Unused, method consistency. data data frame one rating per row. score, subject, rater Columns data (unquoted): numeric rating, subject (object measurement), rater (judge). model Design. "twoway" currently supported. type Error definition. "agreement" (absolute agreement) currently supported; "consistency" planned. unit One "single" (-> ICC(,1)) "average" (-> ICC(,k)). engine Estimation engine. "glmmTMB" currently supported. conf_level Confidence level interval (default 0.95). ci_method Interval method. "montecarlo" currently supported. mc_samples Number Monte-Carlo draws interval. seed Optional integer seed reproducible interval. global RNG state restored afterward.

title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
text: icc object: list estimate table, variance components, design, engine, interval settings, sample sizes, fitted model, call. Use tidy(), glance(), print/summary methods.

what: Which ICC is this, and when should you use it?
title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
text: two-way random, absolute-agreement ICC. Absolute agreement treats systematic differences raters (rater main effect, \(\sigma^2_r\)) error: use actual value matters raters must agree number (clinical scores, measurements). sibling, consistency, ignores constant rater offset yet implemented. ICC(,1) reliability single randomly chosen rater; ICC(,k) reliability mean k raters. Report ICC(,k) averaged score actually use. Two-way random means subjects raters random samples wish generalize beyond. large gap consistency absolute agreement signals big systematic differences rater level – rating-procedure problem worth fixing.

title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
text: single rating per subject--rater cell, subject--rater interaction pure error separately identified; sum, residual variance \(\sigma^2_{res}\), estimable. coefficients $$ICC(,1) = \sigma^2_s / (\sigma^2_s + \sigma^2_r + \sigma^2_{res})$$ $$ICC(,k) = \sigma^2_s / (\sigma^2_s + (\sigma^2_r + \sigma^2_{res}) / k)$$ \(\sigma^2_s\) subject (signal) variance k number raters.

title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
text: Intervals Monte-Carlo: parameters drawn fitted covariance model's internal (log) scale back-transformed, interval boundary-aware near common zero-rater-variance case delta method fails. Pass seed reproducible interval.

title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
text: McGraw, K. O., & Wong, S. P. (1996). Forming inferences intraclass correlation coefficients. Psychological Methods, 1(1), 30-46. Shrout, P. E., & Fleiss, J. L. (1979). Intraclass correlations: uses assessing rater reliability. Psychological Bulletin, 86(2), 420-428.

title: Intraclass correlation coefficient for a two-way random design — format.icc
code: ratings <- data.frame(   subject = factor(rep(1:6, 4)),   rater = factor(rep(1:4, each = 6)),   score = c(9, 6, 8, 7, 10, 6, 2, 1, 4, 1, 5, 2,             5, 3, 6, 2, 6, 4, 8, 2, 8, 6, 9, 7) ) icc(ratings, score, subject, rater, seed = 1) #> # Intraclass correlation: two-way random, absolute agreement #> Subjects: 6 | Raters: 4 (random) | Observations: 24 #> Engine: glmmTMB (REML) | CI: 95% montecarlo (10000 draws) #>   index     estimate   95% CI #>   ICC(A,1)    0.290   [0.050, 0.712] #>   ICC(A,k)    0.620   [0.173, 0.908] #> Variance components: subject 2.556, rater 5.244, residual 1.019

what: intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients — intraclass-package
title: intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients — intraclass-package
text: Estimates interrater-reliability intraclass correlation coefficients (ICCs) within generalizability-theory framework using modern variance-component estimation (linear mixed models) rather classical ANOVA mean-squares approach. Provides full ICC family (absolute agreement versus consistency, single versus average, fixed versus random raters) boundary-aware Monte-Carlo confidence intervals, support imbalanced incomplete designs, guidance choosing correct coefficient.

title: intraclass: Modern Intraclass Correlation Coefficients — intraclass-package
text: Maintainer: Jeffrey Girard jeffgirard@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Jeffrey Girard jeffgirard@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: intraclass 0.0.0.9000
text: icc() computes two-way random, absolute-agreement intraclass correlation coefficients ICC(,1) ICC(,k) glmmTMB linear mixed model, boundary-aware Monte-Carlo confidence intervals, print/summary/format methods tidy()/glance(). Verified Shrout & Fleiss (1979) worked example, psych::ICC, package-independent ANOVA mean-squares oracle, seeded simulation, lme4 cross-check. Project scaffolding: package skeleton, project/ tracking system (principles, milestones, decisions, oracle registry), custom skills agent, CI workflows (R-CMD-check, coverage, lint, format, pkgdown, scheduled reference-values), pkgdown site.
